description: Figure 9 presents scanning electron microscopy (SEM) micrographs of the cross-section of Fe–20Mn–9Al–1.5C–2Ni–3Cr low-density steel after multi-stage heat treatment (MHT) incorporating an intermediate heat treatment at 750 °C. Panel (a), at lower magnification (scale bar 10 μm), shows equiaxed grains with a network of grain boundaries decorated by dark-contrast phases; a dashed white rectangle indicates the region enlarged in panel (b). Panel (b) offers a higher-magnification view (scale bar 2 μm) of the localized area, revealing closely spaced, discontinuous precipitates situated along the grain boundaries while grain interiors appear relatively clean. Note that the caption refers to (b) as low magnification, yet the scale bar confirms it is a magnified view of the boxed region in (a). These micrographs directly evidence extensive intergranular precipitation resulting from the 750 °C intermediate step. As the text reports, these grain-boundary precipitates elevate stress in non-twinned regions, generate more twin nucleation sites, and increase the frequency of special boundaries that disrupt random high-angle grain boundaries, thereby playing a key role in improving impact toughness while preserving strength and ductility.
